name: Adapting Recipes to Pressure Cooking
recipeInstructions:
1. From the Innova Pressure Cooker booklet, 1995:
- Follow the general directions for your recipe.
- Reduce the cooking time by two-thirds, as pressure cooking requires only one-third of the time needed for conventional methods.
2. Additional information from "Cooking Under Pressure":
- For dishes requiring very little liquid:
a. Place ingredients in a souffle dish or suitable heatproof container (e.g., line the steaming basket with aluminum foil).
b. Eliminate water as a cooking ingredient, or reduce to 1/4 cup if using wine, broth, or juice.
- Add the minimum amount of water required by the manufacturer (try 1-2 cups for unknown amounts), placing it at the bottom of the cooker.
- Set a trivet or rack over the water and place the dish on top.
- Secure the lid and adjust the cooking time by reducing it by two-thirds. Additional high pressure may be needed to account for the added liquid.
